title: The Palpatones
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Band |image=[[File:Greeata_btm.jpg]] |name=The Palpatones
{{C|Future lead singer Greeata Jendowanian depicted}} |genre= |founder= |leader=[[Greeata Jendowanian/Legends|Greeata Jendowanian]] {{C|Lead [[Musician/Legends|singer]]}}{{InsiderCite|67|Who's Who in the Max Rebo Band}} |instruments= |songs="[[Kick the Ranat]]"{{SW|url=news/slugthrowers-part-2|series=Slugthrowers: An Overview of Popular Music and Musicians in a Galaxy Far, Far Away|text=Part 2|nolive=1}} |albums= |locations=[[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]]''[[Star Wars Trilogy Sourcebook, Special Edition]]'' |headquarters= |formed=[[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] or later{{SW|url=news/slugthrowers-part-2|series=Slugthrowers: An Overview of Popular Music and Musicians in a Galaxy Far, Far Away|text=Part 2|nolive=1}} confirms [[Joh Yowza/Legends|Joh Yowza]], [[Rystáll Sant/Legends|Rystáll Sant]], and [[Greeata Jendowanian/Legends|Greeata Jendowanian]] formed The Palpatones after the [[Death/Legends|death]] of [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt]], which was established to be [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] in ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. |fragmented= |reorganized= |dissolved= |restored= |affiliation= }} '''The Palpatones''' was a [[Band/Legends|band]] formed following [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY's]] [[Battle of the Great Pit of Carkoon]]. Following the battle, [[Joh Yowza/Legends|Joh Yowza]], who had performed with the [[Max Rebo Band/Legends|Max Rebo Band]], fled to a small [[Passenger liner/Legends|passenger liner]] in the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine's]] [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]]. Aboard the [[Starship/Legends|liner]], Yowza started The Palpatones with two other former Max Rebo Band members, [[Rystáll Sant/Legends|Rystáll Sant]] and [[Greeata Jendowanian/Legends|Greeata Jendowanian]], the latter of whom became the lead [[Musician/Legends|singer]]. A rival to the Max Rebo Band, The Palpatones enjoyed limited success in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] with their hit [[Music/Legends|single]] "[[Kick the Ranat]]," which also helped them become an [[The galaxy/Legends|intergalactic]] sensation. In [[Time/Legends|time]], Sant would leave the group to seek clues to her [[Rystáll Sant's father|father's]] identity. ==History== [[File:Joh.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Joh Yowza, who started The Palpatones with Rystáll Sant and Greeata Jendowanian]] In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine's]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt]] was [[Death/Legends|killed]] in the [[Battle of the Great Pit of Carkoon]].''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' Following Jabba's death, the [[Yuzzum/Legends|Yuzzum]] [[Joh Yowza/Legends|Joh Yowza]], a [[Musician/Legends|musician]] who had worked for the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]], fled to a small [[Passenger liner/Legends|passenger liner]] in Tatooine's [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]]. Yowza, along with the half-[[Theelin/Legends|Theelin]] [[Rystáll Sant/Legends|Rystáll Sant]] and [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Greeata Jendowanian/Legends|Greeata Jendowanian]], formed The Palpatones, a house [[Band/Legends|band]] aboard the [[Starship/Legends|liner]]. The Palpatones were a rival band to the members' previous group, the [[Max Rebo Band/Legends|Max Rebo Band]]. With The Palpatones, Yowza sang in exchange for transportation to new, exotic locales. Furthermore, Yowza was given free berth and specific passage to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], where the band enjoyed limited success with their hit [[Music/Legends|single]] "[[Kick the Ranat]]." Jendowanian, who became the band's lead singer, helped the band become an [[The galaxy/Legends|intergalactic]] sensation through her beautiful voice in performing "Kick the Ranat" and became the band's biggest breakout star. At some point during Sant's [[Time/Legends|time]] with The Palpatones, she met fellow half-Theelin [[Shug Ninx]]. With the thought of her unknown [[Rystáll Sant's father|father]] also being among the last of her [[Species/Legends|species]], Sant left The Palpatones to further seek clues to her [[Parent/Legends|father's]] identity. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:PalpatineGuards-NEGTC.png|thumb|right|200px|Emperor Palpatine, on whose name The Palpatones was a pun]] The Palpatones was introduced, unnamed, in [[1997]]'s ''[[Star Wars Trilogy Sourcebook, Special Edition]]'', a supplement for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. The band received its name in "[[Who's Who in the Max Rebo Band]]," an article [[Rich Handley]] wrote for the [[Star Wars Insider 67|sixty-seventh issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' magazine, which was published on [[April 15]], [[2003]].{{SWArchive|url=community/news/2003/03/news20030317.html|text=Celebrate Jedi's 20th in ''Insider'' #67}} He had previously tried to introduce the name, a pun on [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], in writings for West End Games, but editors there completely refused. [[Dave Gross]], an editor at ''Star Wars Insider'', found the pun to be less objectionable and, to Handley's surprise, allowed the reference to make the final cut.{{Facebook|username=rich.handley.3|url=rich.handley.3/posts/10223283778555809|name=[[Rich Handley]]|date=June 16, 2021 11:45|quote=Here's one of my contributions to the Star Wars franchise that I'm particularly proud of: The Palpatones|archivefile=File:Palpatones-Dahlney-Pollard.png}} While "Who's Who in the Max Rebo Band" and [[2008]]'s ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' refer to the band as "the Palpatones,"{{CSWECite|II|155|Jendowanian, Greeata}} [[Edward M. Erdelac]]'s [[January 21]], [[2014]] [[StarWars.com]] article "[[Slugthrowers: An Overview of Popular Music and Musicians in a Galaxy Far, Far Away]], Part 2" refers to them as "The Palpatones," which is the capitalization used by this article. Further, while "Who's Who in the Max Rebo Band" and ''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia'' claim that the members joined the band, "Slugthrowers: An Overview of Popular Music and Musicians in a Galaxy Far, Far Away, Part 2" claims that they formed the band, which this article assumes to be correct. ==Sources== *''[[Star Wars Trilogy Sourcebook, Special Edition]]'' {{1stm}} *{{InsiderCite|67|Who's Who in the Max Rebo Band}} {{1stID|The Palpatones}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{DB|character|johyowza|Yowza, Joh}} {{Imo}} *{{SW|url=news/slugthrowers-part-2|series=Slugthrowers: An Overview of Popular Music and Musicians in a Galaxy Far, Far Away|text=Part 2|nolive=1}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=The Palpatones |nl=Palpatones }} [[Category:Bands|Palpatones, The]]
